source_fact_summary: The public issue says a code-agent fix added Elasticsearch validator tests, including SSL testing-mode cases that only exercised mock credentials and never constructed the URL or checked protocol selection. The source says those cases would still pass if the protocol fix were reverted.
unsupported_claims: The source also notes that other parsing tests were meaningful, so the whole PR should not be treated as entirely weak.
category_change_reason: No category change suggested: the issue specifically describes tests that appear to validate SSL behavior but do not exercise the code path under change.
privacy_findings: Only public GitHub issue or PR links and short factual summaries are retained; no credentials, private files, raw logs, or local absolute paths were copied.
severity_reason: The weak tests could let a protocol-selection regression pass, but the source distinguishes them from stronger parsing coverage.
missing_information: Manual review should inspect the test diff and decide whether to model only the weak SSL testing-mode assertions.
scope_reason: The case is a target-project code-delivery failure where added tests do not validate the runtime path the Agent changed. Reverting the fix or mutating protocol selection while running the new tests can independently detect the weakness.
notes: Codex review suggestion only: public-source accessibility and category/scope recommendations do not mark the candidate source_verified, approved, or reproduced. Manual confirmation remains required.
